summary: Audience (AUD) source captures a fiery 1973 concert with an explosive second set, but the sound is notably cloudy.
review: The show is widely praised for its high energy – especially the nonstop second set that many reviewers call “blasting off” and note Jerry, Bob and Phil were on top of their game. The recording is an audience tape; listeners love the atmosphere it conveys, yet several point out that the audio is far from pristine, describing it as cloudy or poor for road‑trip listening.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the musical performance highly (average ~4.7) while the audio quality receives mixed feedback (average ~3.0). Weighted combination (70% show, 30% recording) yields a overall rating around 4.1.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Atmospheric crowd ambience is captured well, but the tape is described as cloudy, muffled and not suitable for critical listening; no pristine SBD quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid, second set praised as nonstop high‑energy fire that carries the concert to a strong finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Several reviewers highlight Jerry’s playing as being “on top of it” throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil is noted for staying on point and contributing strongly to the performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob receives positive mention, keeping the momentum alive through the second set.
